summary: See clues file for full details. 6 tossups, 3 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 6 tossups and 3 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: The artist intended this figure to represent Adam, according to a John-Paul Stonard essay which notes that this figure first appeared in a work attributed to "Bruce of Los Angeles." In a later adaptation of the work in which this figure appears, the analogue of this figure sits next to a bust of Margaret Thatcher captioned "Jeff Koons" in a room in which the planet Jupiter hangs from the ceiling.
examples: The artist intended this figure to represent Adam, according to a John-Paul Stonard essay which notes that this figure first appeared in a work attributed to "Bruce of Los Angeles." In a later adaptation of the work in which this figure appears, the analogue of this figure sits next to a bust of Margaret Thatcher captioned "Jeff Koons" in a room in which the planet Jupiter hangs from the ceiling.

clues.clue: In that adaptation, made with the Quantel Paintbox system, this figure is replaced with an (*) accountant sitting at a computer.
examples: In that adaptation, made with the Quantel Paintbox system, this figure is replaced with an (*) accountant sitting at a computer.

clues.clue: This figure stands in front of a staircase where a black arrow saying "ordinary cleaners reach only this far" points to a vacuum hose.
examples: This figure stands in front of a staircase where a black arrow saying "ordinary cleaners reach only this far" points to a vacuum hose.

clues.clue: This figure is taken from a photograph in Tomorrow's Man magazine depicting Irvin Koszweski.
examples: This figure is taken from a photograph in Tomorrow's Man magazine depicting Irvin Koszweski.

clues.clue: A lollipop with the word "pop" on it is held by, for 10 points, what figure in Richard Hamilton's collage Just what is it that makes today's homes so different, so appealing?
examples: A lollipop with the word "pop" on it is held by, for 10 points, what figure in Richard Hamilton's collage Just what is it that makes today's homes so different, so appealing?

clues.clue: This artist created a collage of pictures of Sophia Loren's features cut out from magazines in Fashion Plate.
examples: This artist created a collage of pictures of Sophia Loren's features cut out from magazines in Fashion Plate.

clues.clue: This artist used an "an anthology of presentation techniques" in a painting in which the outline of a woman with red lips blends into the hood of a car.
examples: This artist used an "an anthology of presentation techniques" in a painting in which the outline of a woman with red lips blends into the hood of a car.

clues.clue: One of this artist's works features the planet Jupiter hanging from a ceiling and the word AIDS arranged like Robert Indiana's LOVE.
examples: One of this artist's works features the planet Jupiter hanging from a ceiling and the word AIDS arranged like Robert Indiana's LOVE.

clues.clue: The left side of a diptych by this man has abstract swirls representing (*) feces smeared on the walls of the Maze prison, and forms a series with works depicting an Orangeman and a soldier, called The Subject and The State.
examples: The left side of a diptych by this man has abstract swirls representing (*) feces smeared on the walls of the Maze prison, and forms a series with works depicting an Orangeman and a soldier, called The Subject and The State.

clues.clue: This artist of Hommage a Chrysler Corp. painted Bobby Sands in The Citizen.
examples: This artist of Hommage a Chrysler Corp. painted Bobby Sands in The Citizen.

clues.clue: This artist's most famous work was created for the "This is Tomorrow" exhibition.
examples: This artist's most famous work was created for the "This is Tomorrow" exhibition.

clues.clue: This artist created six relief sculptures that use black, gold, Neapolitan, spectrum, metalflake, and black and white shades to convey the form of the Guggenheim Museum.
examples: This artist created six relief sculptures that use black, gold, Neapolitan, spectrum, metalflake, and black and white shades to convey the form of the Guggenheim Museum.
